content.text:
Light is amazing — it helps us see everything around us! Let's explore how light works.

☀️ Sources of Light:
Some objects make their own light — these are called LUMINOUS objects:
• The Sun (the biggest source of light for Earth)
• Light bulbs
• Flames and candles
• Fireflies!

Other objects just reflect light — like the Moon and mirrors.

🔦 How does light travel?
Light travels in STRAIGHT LINES — very, very fast! It travels so fast that it can go around Earth 7 times in just one second!

🌈 What are shadows?
A shadow forms when an OPAQUE object blocks light. Opaque means light cannot pass through it. Your body blocks light from the sun and makes a shadow behind you.

Transparent objects (like clear glass) let most light through — little or no shadow.
Translucent objects (like frosted glass) let some light through — a faint shadow.
Opaque objects (like a rock) block all light — a dark shadow.

🕐 Shadows change throughout the day:
• Morning: shadow is long, pointing west (sun is in the east)
• Noon: shadow is shortest (sun is overhead)
• Afternoon: shadow is long, pointing east (sun is in the west)

Before clocks existed, people used shadow sticks called SUNDIALS to tell the time!

diagrams.alt: Diagram showing how shadows form and change direction throughout the day

experiment.instructions: Shine the flashlight on the object and watch the shadow form! Try changing the angle.

combinations.resultLabel: A shadow appears behind the block! The opaque block blocks the light from passing through, creating a dark shadow. Light travels in straight lines, so the shadow is the same shape as the block!

practiceQuestions.prompt: Which of these is a LUMINOUS object?
practiceQuestions.hint: Luminous means it makes its own light — which one actually produces light?

practiceQuestions.prompt: When is your shadow the SHORTEST during the day?
practiceQuestions.hint: Think about where the sun is in the sky at noon — and how that affects shadow length.

practiceQuestions.prompt: What kind of object creates a dark shadow?
practiceQuestions.hint: Which type of object completely blocks light from passing through?

questions.prompt: Frosted glass lets some light through but creates a faint shadow. This means frosted glass is:
What is a sundial used for?
In the morning, which direction does your shadow point?
The Moon is NOT a luminous object because:

options.text: It reflects sunlight rather than making its own light
